title: Avoid Using Double Brace Initialization in Java
description: Double Brace Initialization (DBI) in Java results in the creation of an anonymous class with reference to the instance of the owning object. This can potentially lead to memory leaks if the anonymous inner class is returned and held by other objects. It is also a less common practice and can result in confusion for most maintainers.
recommendation: Replace the use of Double Brace Initialization with Arrays.asList or directly add items to the collection.
impact: Using DBI can lead to memory leaks and confuse code maintainers. Fixing this improves code clarity and memory management.

checks.message: Avoid using double brace initialization - it creates anonymous classes that can cause memory leaks
